model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Virginia holds a commanding ~26-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over Wright State (KenPom #13, AdjEM +26.49, Torvik #14 vs. Wright State's SRS of 0.12, ranked 152nd nationally), with a top-28 offense and top-14 defense built against elite ACC competition that the Horizon League schedule — ranked 242nd in strength of schedule — cannot replicate. The Cavaliers arrive in peak form after a dominant 84-62 ACC Tournament win over Miami and carry a 29-4 record that validates their elite two-way efficiency profile, while Wright State's defense (155th nationally, DRtg 106.0) presents no credible barrier to Virginia's offense. The betting market's 17.5-point opening spread reflects broad consensus on the efficiency gap, and Wright State's Horizon League title — secured by erasing a 12-point second-half deficit against Detroit Mercy — while admirable, does not translate to a viable upset path against a top-15 national program. The historical 3-vs-14 record and this specific efficiency chasm make Virginia's advancement a near-certainty.

reasoningStep.title: Virginia vs. Wright State — Round of 64 Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Virginia is a dominant 3-seed with elite two-way efficiency (KenPom #13, AdjEM +26.49, Torvik #14) facing a Horizon League automatic qualifier (Wright State, SRS 0.12, 152nd nationally) with a weak schedule (SOS 242nd) and a below-average defense (DRtg 106.0, 155th nationally). The 17.5-point betting spread, ~26-point efficiency gap, and Virginia's peak form (29-4, just beat Miami 84-62 in the ACC Tournament) all point overwhelmingly to a Cavaliers victory with no credible upset path for Wright State.
evidence: Virginia: KenPom #13, AdjEM +26.49, AdjOE 122.2 (28th nationally), AdjDE 95.7 (14th nationally), 29-4 record in the ACC.
Wright State: SRS 0.12 (152nd of 365 nationally), SOS -4.44 (242nd), ORtg 116.5 (53rd), DRtg 106.0 (155th), 23-11 overall (15-5 Horizon League). No KenPom or Torvik rating available.
Virginia opened as a 17.5-point favorite over Wright State with a total of 145.5 (Action Network).
Virginia beat Miami 84-62 on March 13 and NC State 81-74 on March 12 in the ACC Tournament, demonstrating strong late-season form.
Wright State won the Horizon League Tournament over Detroit Mercy 66-63 after trailing by 12 — a credible conference title but against competition far below ACC caliber.

result.text: Ticket Punched: Previewing the Wright State Raiders for the 2026 NCAA Tournament Articles Podcasts Scout Team Game Analysis Bracketology Women's Hoops Free Content Team Previews Scoreboard Search Sign Up Sign Up Create Your Account Get access to exclusive features and stay updated. Sign Up Sign Up Already have an account? Log In Articles Podcasts Scout Team Game Analysis Bracketology Women's Hoops Free Content Team Previews Scoreboard Become a member Become a member Home Articles & Editorials Ticket Punched: Previewing the Wright State Raiders for the 2026 NCAA Tournament Articles & Editorials Articles & Editorials Bracketology Bracketology Free Content Free Content Ticket Punched: Previewing the Wright State Raiders for the 2026 NCAA Tournament Featured Mar 12, 2026 by Lukas Harkins Despite trailing by five in the second half of the semifinals and by 11 in the second half of the title game, top-seeded Wright State is headed to the NCAA Tournament as the Horizon League representative... Wright State earned the No. 1 seed at the Horizon League Tournament after a 15-5 intra-league record meant winning the regular-season conference title by two games. After dominating Cleveland State by 29 in the Nutter Center in the quarterfinals, the Raiders made the trip to Indianapolis for the semifinals — less than a month from when the city will host the national semifinals just a few miles down the road. It marked the program's first trip to the neutral-site portion of the Horizon League Tournament since it won the event in 2022. The Raiders did not waste the opportunity, rising to the occasion in second-half comebacks of both games en route to winning the league's automatic bid. First, freshman guard Michael Cooper played hero by scoring 23 of his 25 points after halftime in the semifinal win over Northern Kentucky. Against Detroit Mercy in the title game, it was Logan Woods who capped a 11-point second-half comeback with back-to-back 3-pointers in the final 2:11 to turn a tie game into a six-point lead: Wright State LEADS for the first time all second half! The Raiders trailed by as many as 11. 2:08 left in the Horizon League title game. Wright State up 59-56. pic.twitter.com/iX0sTKe1VO — Lukas Harkins (@hardwiredsports) March 11, 2026 Freshman forward Kellen Pickett swatted three shots in the final 2:29, including two game-sealers in the last 13 seconds. Over the course of the whole event, Wright State's most consistent performer was sophomore guard TJ Burch, who won Tournament MVP honors for averaging 19.0 points, 3.3 rebounds, 3.0 assists, 1.3 steals, and 1.0 blocks per contest. Now heading to the Big Dance for the first time since 2022, let's dive deeper into this iteration of the Raiders and how they could attempt to threaten a top seed. Wright State puts pressure on the rim but has counters. Wright State attempted 43.1% of its total shots at the rim this season, a mark which rates in the 100th percentile by CBB Analytics. While they only rat

result.title: Dedication for Tony Bennett Rescheduled for Feb. 21 - Virginia ...
result.snippet: Bennett retired on Oct. 18, 2024, after posting a 364-136 record in 15 seasons at Virginia from 2009-24. He guided the Cavaliers to the 2019 NCAA Championship, two ACC Tournament titles, six ACC regular-season championships and 10 NCAA tournament appearances. Bennett was named ACC Coach of the Year in 2014, 2015, 2018 and 2019.

result.title: Tony Bennett (basketball) - Wikipedia
result.snippet: In just Bennett's third year at Virginia , he led the Cavaliers to 22 wins and an NCAA Tournament berth. It was the most wins the program had tallied in 17 years and its first NCAA Tournament game (a lopsided loss to Billy Donovan and Florida) in five years.

result.title: Bennett Enjoying Life After Coaching - Virginia Cavaliers Official ...
result.snippet: Bennett Enjoying Life After Coaching In a pregame ceremony Saturday, the court at John Paul Jones Arena will dedicated to former UVA head coach Tony Bennett in honor of his contributions to the men's basketball program, the University and the community.

result.snippet: INDIANAPOLIS - Kellen Pickett blocked an Orlando Lovejoy potential game-tying layup in the closing moments as the Wright State men's basketball team erased a 12-point second-half deficit, defeating the Detroit Mercy 66-63 to capture the 2026 Barbasol Horizon League Championship on Tuesday night ...

result.title: 2025-26 Wright State Raiders men's basketball team - Wikipedia
result.snippet: The 2025-26 Wright State Raiders men's basketball team represents Wright State University in the 2025-26 NCAA Division I men's basketball season. The Raiders , led by second-year head coach Clint Sargent, played their home games at the Nutter Center near Dayton as members of the Horizon League .

arguments.query: Virginia Cavaliers 2026 NCAA Tournament injury news Tony Bennett

result.title: Dedication for Tony Bennett Rescheduled for Feb. 21 - Virginia ...
result.snippet: Bennett retired on Oct. 18, 2024, after posting a 364-136 record in 15 seasons at Virginia from 2009-24. He guided the Cavaliers to the 2019 NCAA Championship, two ACC Tournament titles, six ACC regular-season championships and 10 NCAA tournament appearances. Bennett was named ACC Coach of the Year in 2014, 2015, 2018 and 2019.

result.title: Tony Bennett (basketball) - Wikipedia
result.snippet: In just Bennett's third year at Virginia , he led the Cavaliers to 22 wins and an NCAA Tournament berth. It was the most wins the program had tallied in 17 years and its first NCAA Tournament game (a lopsided loss to Billy Donovan and Florida) in five years.

result.title: Bennett Enjoying Life After Coaching - Virginia Cavaliers Official ...
result.snippet: Bennett Enjoying Life After Coaching In a pregame ceremony Saturday, the court at John Paul Jones Arena will dedicated to former UVA head coach Tony Bennett in honor of his contributions to the men's basketball program, the University and the community.

result.title: Men's Basketball - Virginia Cavaliers Official Athletic Site
result.snippet: The Official Athletic Site of the University of Virginia , partner of WMT Digital. The most comprehensive coverage of the Virginia Cavaliers Athletics on the web with rosters, schedules, scores, highlights, game recaps and more!

modelTrace.content: {"pick":{"gameId":"r64-midwest-6","winnerId":"virginia","confidence":0.88,"rationale":"Virginia holds a commanding ~26-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over Wright State (KenPom #13, AdjEM +26.49, Torvik #14 vs. Wright State's SRS of 0.12, ranked 152nd nationally), with a top-28 offense and top-14 defense built against elite ACC competition that the Horizon League schedule — ranked 242nd in strength of schedule — cannot replicate. The Cavaliers arrive in peak form after a dominant 84-62 ACC Tournament win over Miami and carry a 29-4 record that validates their elite two-way efficiency profile, while Wright State's defense (155th nationally, DRtg 106.0) presents no credible barrier to Virginia's offense. The betting market's 17.5-point opening spread reflects broad consensus on the efficiency gap, and Wright State's Horizon League title — secured by erasing a 12-point second-half deficit against Detroit Mercy — while admirable, does not translate to a viable upset path against a top-15 national program. The historical 3-vs-14 record and this specific efficiency chasm make Virginia's advancement a near-certainty."},"reasoningStep":{"id":"r64-midwest-6-analysis","title":"Virginia vs. Wright State — Round of 64 Matchup Analysis","summary":"Virginia is a dominant 3-seed with elite two-way efficiency (KenPom #13, AdjEM +26.49, Torvik #14) facing a Horizon League automatic qualifier (Wright State, SRS 0.12, 152nd nationally) with a weak schedule (SOS 242nd) and a below-average defense (DRtg 106.0, 155th nationally). The 17.5-point betting spread, ~26-point efficiency gap, and Virginia's peak form (29-4, just beat Miami 84-62 in the ACC Tournament) all point overwhelmingly to a Cavaliers victory with no credible upset path for Wright State.","evidence":["Virginia: KenPom #13, AdjEM +26.49, AdjOE 122.2 (28th nationally), AdjDE 95.7 (14th nationally), 29-4 record in the ACC.","Wright State: SRS 0.12 (152nd of 365 nationally), SOS -4.44 (242nd), ORtg 116.5 (53rd), DRtg 106.0 (155th), 23-11 overall (15-5 Horizon League). No KenPom or Torvik rating available.","Virginia opened as a 17.5-point favorite over Wright State with a total of 145.5 (Action Network).","Virginia beat Miami 84-62 on March 13 and NC State 81-74 on March 12 in the ACC Tournament, demonstrating strong late-season form.","Wright State won the Horizon League Tournament over Detroit Mercy 66-63 after trailing by 12 — a credible conference title but against competition far below ACC caliber."]}}
